stemHtml: Why should Airborne Weather Radar (AWR) systems be used with extreme caution when on the ground?

options.a: The radar system may overheat and shut down due to the intensity of the returns from the ground, buildings, and surrounding terrain.
options.b: People could be exposed to Super High Frequency (SHF) radiation which, if absorbed in large enough amounts, can lead to tissue damage.
options.c: The radar antenna can be easily damaged during ground-taxi manoeuvres (especially on uneven taxiways).
options.d: The intense returns from the ground and surrounding terrain will not allow accurate identification of potentially hazardous weather when lined up for departure.
